title: Paths, Shapes, Distortion & Gravity
description: CSS scroll-driven SVG reveals, shape-outside layouts, feTurbulence text distortion, gravity trails, and :has() OTP inputs.

drops.title: CSS Scroll-Driven SVG Path Reveal
drops.description: Three SVG strokes draw themselves as you scroll — pure CSS, zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: Uses stroke-dasharray/dashoffset on SVG paths, animated via CSS animation-timeline: scroll(). Each path has a different animation-range so they reveal at different scroll depths. No GSAP or JS scroll listeners needed — the browser's scroll timeline handles everything natively.
drops.source: Codrops – Creating Scroll-Driven SVG Map Animations with GSAP (May 2026), reimplemented in native CSS

drops.title: shape-outside Magazine Wrap
drops.description: Text flowing around circles, diamonds, ellipses, and rounded rects using CSS shape-outside.
drops.concept: The shape-outside CSS property tells the browser to wrap inline text around a custom geometric shape rather than a rectangular bounding box. Four variations are shown: circle(), polygon() for a diamond, ellipse(), and inset() with round corners. Resize the window to see text reflow live.
drops.source: CSS Shapes specification (MDN Web Docs / CSS Working Group)

drops.title: SVG feTurbulence Text Distortion
drops.description: Hover LIQUID, SHATTER, GLITCH or MELT to trigger animated SVG filter distortion on each word.
drops.concept: An SVG feTurbulence filter generates a noise field; feDisplacementMap uses that noise to offset each pixel's coordinates, warping the text. Animating the feDisplacementMap's scale attribute from 0 to a peak value (via requestAnimationFrame spring) creates the liquid/shatter/glitch/melt illusion. Each word has a different baseFrequency, numOctaves, and turbulence type.
drops.source: SVG Filter Effects specification (W3C) — classic creative coding technique

interaction.mouse: Hover to warp; click to hold

drops.title: Gravity Particle Cursor Trail
drops.description: Particles spawn at cursor speed, fall under gravity, drift with wind, and fade out beautifully.
drops.concept: Each frame, particles are spawned along the cursor path with velocity proportional to movement speed. They accumulate downward velocity each frame (gravity) and sideward drift (wind), then are removed when their lifetime expires. Five particle shapes (circle, square, star, triangle, ring) and five color palettes are randomly assigned. The canvas uses a semi-transparent clear (alpha 0.18) to create a trailing blur effect.
drops.source: Inspired by Codrops 'Made With GSAP: Building a Fun Gravity-Based Mouse Trail' (May 2026), reimplemented in vanilla Canvas 2D

interaction.mouse: Move to spawn particles; click for burst
interaction.touch: Touch drag to spawn; tap for burst

drops.title: CSS :has() OTP / PIN Input
drops.description: Progress bar and validation colors driven by CSS :has() — no JavaScript state observers.
drops.concept: The :has() pseudo-class lets a parent element react to the state of its children. Here, .otp-wrap:has(.otp-input:nth-child(N):not(:placeholder-shown)) selects the wrapper when the Nth input has a value (not showing placeholder), and drives the progress bar width and border colours through pure CSS. JS only handles keyboard navigation (focus chain, backspace), not the visual validation state.
drops.source: CSS :has() pseudo-class (CSS Selectors Level 4) — widely adopted pattern in 2025-2026 component libraries
